topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on the screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet afternoon. Liam froze, his hand hovering over the phone. It was a login alert, something he’d never seen before. Curiosity warred with a sudden, sharp tightening in his chest. He navigated to the profile, and the carefully curated persona of his daughter—the thoughtful, slightly reserved student—vanished, replaced by a cascade of unfiltered, almost reckless posts. A wave of heat washed over him, prickling the back of his neck. He felt a profound sense of having missed something crucial, a wall he hadn't seen standing up. He quickly closed the app, his breathing shallow, the silence of the room suddenly heavy with unspoken awareness.

[story 2]

"Mom, can I just use the Wi-Fi?" Maya asked, her tone overly bright, a practiced nonchalance that felt brittle. When Liam saw the unfamiliar icon flash across her tablet screen—a profile picture he didn't recognize, a stream of cryptic, late-night updates—a cold dread settled in his stomach. He watched her fingers expertly swipe away the window, a quick, almost furtive motion. He stood there, rooted to the spot, the air in the living room seeming to thicken. He wanted to speak, to ask, but the words caught in his throat, lodging themselves as heavy stones. He just stared at the blank screen, his jaw clenching so tightly his temples throbbed.

[story 3]

I found the password easily enough, a weak combination I’d forgotten she’d used months ago. As the profile loaded, the sheer volume of activity hit me like a physical blow. It wasn't just pictures; it was conversations, shared moments that felt miles away from the reality of our home. A tremor ran through my hands as I scrolled through the content, each interaction a small, sharp sting. I retreated quickly, pulling my chair back as if the screen were suddenly hot. I felt a profound sense of inadequacy, a sickening realization that I hadn't been paying attention, that I hadn't seen the landscape she was navigating in secret. I stood by the doorway, listening to the muffled sounds from her room, my entire posture radiating a deep, uncomfortable self-reproach.
